signal_breakdown.explanation: Already operating exactly like Hotplate. Text alert list drives demand, orders close fast. The friction is the manual text/DM collection — Hotplate automates that.
Running a manual waitlist means overflow demand they cannot capture. Hotplate's preorder window replaces this.
Solid local audience in the sweet spot. Engaged enough that drops sell through, not so large that manual tools can keep up.
Nutella pistachio, cinnamon roll, chocolate chip — changing menu gives regulars a reason to check every drop.
Pure cottage operation. No lease overhead means switching to Hotplate is low risk and the economics work immediately.

nocrumbscookies.recommended_action: Top outreach target. Lead with eliminating the manual text list — Hotplate automates waitlists, payments, and pickup confirmation in one place.

signal_breakdown.explanation: Daily sellouts mean demand is real and consistent. The constraint is the in-person queue model, not buyer interest.
Strong audience well in the sweet spot. The Infatuation coverage adds credibility and discovery.
Operating entirely in-person with no way to capture orders in advance. Hotplate adds a preorder layer without changing the pickup model.

signal_breakdown.signal: Popup at Paper Son Coffee (venue dependency)
signal_breakdown.explanation: Venue partnership is a constraint — if Paper Son changes terms, the business is exposed. A Hotplate storefront creates a direct customer relationship.

signal_breakdown.signal: Unique product with no SF equivalent
signal_breakdown.explanation: Korean salt bread with ube cream is a differentiated product. Loyal customers would follow to a new ordering platform.

tano__sf.recommended_action: Pitch Hotplate as a preorder layer that captures the customers who cannot make it Wed-Fri. They are leaving money on the table every week.

signal_breakdown.explanation: Already running preorders manually through Instagram DMs. Hotplate is the same flow, automated.
Strong neighborhood following with no storefront overhead. Classic cottage food profile.
Near-weekly cadence signals a serious operation, not a hobby. That consistency is the foundation for a Hotplate storefront.
Eclairs and tartlettes command higher prices than cookies. Higher AOV means Hotplate's flat fee model is proportionally better than Square percentages.
Zero infrastructure outside Instagram. Hotplate adds a permanent storefront with no extra work.

patisserieoncalifornia.recommended_action: Lead with replacing the DM preorder chaos with a clean link customers can bookmark. The setup takes 10 minutes.

signal_breakdown.explanation: Sourdough-leavened cookies are a specific niche with a dedicated SF audience. Not competing with generic cookie accounts.
Manual DM collection is the right signal. Switch motivation exists once they know a solution exists.
Audience is growing but not yet at the scale where manual ordering becomes a real burden. Switch motivation is lower urgency.
Without a weekly rhythm, the urgency to switch platforms is lower. Cadence needs to build before Hotplate is the obvious next step.

apfloursf.recommended_action: Warm outreach. Share a Hotplate setup link rather than pitching hard. Let them try it when the next drop gets chaotic.

signal_breakdown.signal: Square preorder site with rotating flavors
signal_breakdown.explanation: Already e-commerce enabled for preorders. Switch case is about reducing fees and matching the drop format better.
Mission is a high-density SF neighborhood for food popups. Strong local customer base.
Growing audience. Preorder model is right but Square's transaction fees cut into margin on a $5-8 concha.

signal_breakdown.signal: Seasonal rotating flavors drive repeat visits
signal_breakdown.explanation: New flavors every drop create urgency and repeat buyer behavior — the drop model's core engine.
Square works but is not purpose-built for drops. The pitch needs to focus on fee savings and UX fit.

florecitapanaderia.recommended_action: Lead with the fee comparison and the drop-format fit. Calculate their monthly Square costs against Hotplate's structure.

signal_breakdown.explanation: Manual DM ordering is the core friction Hotplate eliminates. Every drop requires individual messages.
24-hour story windows means customers who miss the story miss the drop. Hotplate's preorder window captures everyone.
Solid audience in the sweet spot. Enough demand to make the DM chaos real, not enough infrastructure to absorb it.

signal_breakdown.signal: Fresh mochi niche — loyal repeat buyers
signal_breakdown.explanation: Handmade fresh mochi is a specialty product with a dedicated customer base that will follow to a new platform.

jiaqis_mochi.recommended_action: Lead with the story ordering problem. Customers who miss the story miss the drop entirely. Hotplate captures them all.

signal_breakdown.explanation: Consistent weekly rhythm with advance preorders is exactly the Hotplate drop model. Already operating this way manually.
Strong audience with proven demand. Covered by The Infatuation, which drives new customer discovery.
Already has a working preorder system. Switch motivation is about reducing Shopify fees and simplifying the flow, not escaping chaos.
Community-rooted operation with a neighborhood following. Loyal repeat buyers are the foundation.
Limited availability means constrained supply — which is actually the right setup for Hotplate's preorder drop model.

cometochristines.recommended_action: Lead with the fee comparison and UX simplification. They already have the drop behavior. Hotplate is just a better-fitted tool.

signal_breakdown.signal: Catering and private events only, DM for access
signal_breakdown.explanation: No public-facing storefront means every customer comes through Instagram DMs. That is exactly the friction Hotplate replaces.
Solid following in the sweet spot. Demand exists but the access mechanism is purely manual.
Popup-only model with no venue dependency makes a Hotplate switch low-risk. No lease to protect.
Differentiated product with a specific customer who actively seeks them out. That loyalty transfers to a new ordering platform.

kayabakerysf.recommended_action: Pitch Hotplate as a public-facing storefront that replaces the DM inbox. Customers can find and order without having to DM first.

signal_breakdown.explanation: Already running preorders. The behavior is right but a website-based system means they have partial infrastructure.
Physical location reduces the urgency to switch. Hotplate is a supplementary channel for them, not a primary one.
Multi-channel presence (cafe, farmers market, website) means the operation is established. Switch motivation is lower.
Moving toward wholesale reduces the direct-to-consumer focus that makes Hotplate most compelling.

bernal_bakery.recommended_action: Lower priority. They have a working system. If pitching, frame Hotplate as a drops channel alongside their cafe, not a replacement.

signal_breakdown.explanation: Large following but fully brick-and-mortar. Walk-in model means Hotplate's drop format is not the right fit for their current operation.

signal_breakdown.signal: Started as home delivery, now permanent
signal_breakdown.explanation: Graduated from the cottage model. The switch Hotplate is designed for already happened when they signed a lease.
Well-established in the SF food scene. Not a prospect for the current acquisition strategy.

jinabakes.recommended_action: Not a current fit. Brick-and-mortar with an established walk-in model. Flag if they ever open a secondary drops channel.

signal_breakdown.explanation: Graduated from popup to brick-and-mortar. The window for a Hotplate pitch was before they signed the lease in NoPa.
Strong brand but the daily walk-in model is now established. Hotplate's drop format does not map to their current operation.

signal_breakdown.signal: Previously a popup — ideal past profile
signal_breakdown.explanation: Six months ago this would have been a top-5 target. The lesson is to identify and reach popups before they graduate to storefronts.

sol___bakery.recommended_action: Not a current fit — just opened a storefront. Use as a case study for why early identification matters: catch them before the lease.

signal_breakdown.explanation: 16-year-old business with a permanent market stall. Not a cottage food switcher.

signal_breakdown.signal: Online ordering and delivery already operational
signal_breakdown.explanation: Fully e-commerce enabled with their own ordering site. No clear pain point for Hotplate to solve.
Multi-location operation well beyond the indie maker profile Hotplate is optimized for.

elportenosf.recommended_action: Not a fit. Skip entirely. Established multi-location operation with no drop-format use case.

signal_breakdown.explanation: The behavior is right but the scale is too small. Manual DM ordering at 154 followers is manageable.
Well below the threshold where operational chaos justifies a platform switch. Early stage.
One drop per month means low operational pressure. The pain point that drives switching has not appeared yet.

chocolateboybakery.recommended_action: Watch and revisit at 2k followers. The habit is there but the scale is not.
